# Account Development Representative

London, England, United Kingdom

At PitchBook, a Morningstar company, we are always looking forward. We continue to innovate, evolve, and invest in ourselves to bring out the best in everyone. We’re deeply collaborative and thrive on the excitement, energy, and fun that reverberates throughout the company. 

Our extensive learning programs and mentorship opportunities help us create a culture of curiosity that pushes us to always find new solutions and better ways of doing things. The combination of a rapidly evolving industry and our high ambitions means there’s going to be some ambiguity along the way, but we excel when we challenge ourselves. We’re willing to take risks, fail fast, and do it all over again in the pursuit of excellence.

If you have a good attitude and are willing to roll up your sleeves to get things done, PitchBook is the place for you.

**About the Role:**

As a member of our Account Management & Customer Success team, you will thrive on your ability to embrace and drive change and your commitment to ensuring you and PitchBook are always growing and improving. While continually learning and exploring new ideas and concepts, you will be inspired to ask questions and challenge the status quo while remaining focused on our customers’ success. We value teamwork and collaboration and encourage our people to build strong, trusting relationships with their teammates, customers, and partners. A contagious positivity, insatiable curiosity, and people-first attitude are the winning traits of our most successful Account Management & Customer Success team members. If this sounds like you, join us!

PitchBook’s Account Development Representatives (ADRs) are responsible for generating qualified opportunities and driving early-stage pipeline within Core accounts. Account Development Representatives work within a fixed book of accounts, partnering with Account Managers (AMs) and Business Development Representatives (BDRs) to uncover growth within existing clients and identify new opportunities across target territories.

This role is primarily outbound prospecting, with some inbound lead follow-up, and a strong focus on discovery and qualification to deliver high-quality opportunities that fuel revenue growth. While Account Development Representatives don’t close deals, they play a critical role in advancing opportunities and enabling account teams to achieve sales goals through collaboration and personalized outreach. Success in this role directly contributes to company growth by strengthening the sales pipeline and building strong client relationships, creating a foundation for career advancement.

**Primary Job Responsibilities:**

* Drive outbound prospecting and manage inbound account leads to create qualified opportunities for Account Managers and Business Development Representatives, ensuring a steady flow of high-quality pipeline that supports sales goals
* Conduct discovery calls and thoroughly qualify prospects before handoff to ensure strong fit and conversion potential
* Meet or exceed monthly and quarterly quotas for qualified opportunities and pipeline contribution
* Work with Account Managers, Business Development Representatives, and Customer Success Managers on a fixed book of accounts to build pipeline and drive growth through coordinated strategies
* Support stalled opportunities by expanding account engagement and nurturing momentum through the funnel
* Research accounts and territories to uncover new contacts, decision-makers, and growth opportunities
* Use tools like LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Outreach.io, and Highspot to craft tailored messaging that resonates with prospects
* Maintain accurate records in Salesforce to ensure visibility and smooth progression of opportunities
* Review interactions in Gong, share insights, and refine strategies to improve effectiveness
* Build expertise in PitchBook’s offerings and the private capital markets to deliver value in every interaction
* Actively contribute to our company’s vision and culture, serving as a role model and helping to create a positive, high-performing team environment
* Support the vision and values of the company through role modeling and encouraging desired behaviors
* Participate in various company initiatives and projects as requested

**Skills and Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree
* 1+ years of outbound prospecting experience
* A client first attitude and love to engage prospective new users for account growth
* Prior experience with Salesforce or similar CRM preferred
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills with a keen eye for detail
* Interested in financial markets or services, particularly private equity and venture capital
* Ability to operate with a strong sense of urgency and deliver results
* Terrific prioritization skills to manage your sales pipeline
* Comfortable to engage with a diverse array of clients
* Tenacious and goal-oriented; can work effectively as part of a group
* Team player with the desire to try new ideas in order to achieve greater levels of success
* Proficiency with the Microsoft Office suite including in-depth knowledge of Outlook, Word, and Excel with the ability to pick up new systems and software easily
* Must be authorized to work in the United Kingdom without the need for visa sponsorship now or in the future

**Benefits at PitchBook:**

Physical Health            

* Private medical insurance
* Dental scheme
* Additional medical wellness incentives
* Life cover

Emotional Health 

* Paid sabbatical program after four years
* Paid parental leave
* Education subsidies
* Robust training programs on industry and soft skills
* Minimum 25 days annual leave and volunteer days

Social Health 

* Employee resource groups
* Company-wide events
* Employee referral bonus program
* Quarterly team building events

Financial Health 

* 10% Pension contribution
* Income protection
* Shared ownership employee stock program
* Transportation stipend

*\*Please be aware the above PitchBook benefit and perk offerings are subject to corresponding plan and policy documents and may change during the course of your employment.*

**Working Conditions:**

At the heart of our company is a belief in the power of in-person collaboration. Being together in the office fuels our creativity, strengthens our connections, and drives the innovation that sets us apart. Our culture is built on spontaneous moments—those hallway conversations, whiteboard brainstorms, and shared celebrations in each of our global offices—that simply can’t be replicated remotely. This role is expected to be in the office 5 days a week.

The job conditions for this position are in a standard office setting. Employees in this position use PC and phone on an on-going basis throughout the day. Limited corporate travel may be required to remote offices or other business meetings and events.

# Account Director

Jun 26, 2026

**About WPP**

WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. We unite cutting-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world-class creativity, next-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel in a single company – powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open, to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth. For more information, visit WPP.com.

Step into the forefront of marketing brilliance as our next Account Director, where your strategic vision and commercial acumen will shape the future of global brand experiences. In this role, you’ll unlock the power of cutting-edge CRM systems and high-impact campaigns, catalysing growth for some of the world’s most recognizable global brands. If you thrive on managing complex, large-scale client relationships, have a passion for data-driven customer journeys, and possess a deep understanding of premium brand ecosystems, your leadership is the missing ingredient in our recipe for success.

## What will your day look like?

We are seeking a highly commercially minded Account Director to drive CRM strategy, stakeholder management, and the growth of our global marketing operations. You will be the trusted advisor to major global clients, guiding them on how to optimize customer engagement, loyalty, and lifetime value through sophisticated CRM initiatives.

You will lead the client roadmap, orchestrate multi-channel campaign strategies, and oversee a dedicated delivery team. In this role, you must be a master of data-driven decision-making, possess a deep understanding of CRM best practices, and excel at managing senior stakeholders on a global scale.

More specifically, your tasks include:

* Leading and developing (CRM) and loyalty initiatives for major global brands.

* Focusing on enhancing sophisticated, personalized client communication strategies.

* Managing global client relationships, acting as a strategic partner and driving growth and organic business development.

* Developing project scopes, complex plans, and budgets, ensuring commercial viability and profitability.

* Producing high-quality presentations for executive-level client meetings.

* Team leadership; directly managing and mentoring a team of project managers.

* Cultivating a high-performing team culture, defining clear roles, objectives, and career growth paths.

## Who are you going to work with?

You will be the linchpin in a network of closely coordinated project managers, developers, data scientists, and experienced designers. You will work alongside competent teammates from all around the world who always go the extra mile to help one another. We are a flat organization that loves growing the talents of our people, and many of our team members have been with the agency for a decade or longer.

## What do you bring to the table?

We are on the lookout for a senior, strategic-minded individual with a proven track record of managing large-scale global accounts. You are proactive in initiating campaigns that not only meet but surpass client expectations regularly. You are an easy-going person with a proactive, positive mindset and the resilience to navigate the complexities of global matrix organizations. As you will be overseeing several workstreams simultaneously, you are highly organized and can keep the big picture in focus while showing great attention to detail.

In addition, you have:

* 8+ years of experience in Account Management or Project Management within CRM, digital marketing, or a related agency environment.

* Proven experience working with large global brands (experience in the luxury and/or automotive sectors is a massive advantage).

* Extensive experience leading teams and managing senior stakeholders across complex, global departments.

* Deep knowledge of what working with data, technical projects, and enterprise marketing automation platforms entails.

* Experience with the strategic management and implementation of IT projects within CRM, web, and/or marketing automation.

* Strong hands-on familiarity with enterprise platforms, ideally Salesforce Marketing Cloud or Adobe Marketing Cloud.

* The competence, confidence, and self-discipline to manage deadlines, estimates, and budgets excellently.

* Exceptional communication and presentation skills in English.

## A leader in personalized customer experiences

VML MAP is a world-leading Centre of Excellence that helps businesses humanize the relationship between the brand and the customer through hyper personalization at scale, marketing automation and CRM. With the brain of a consultancy, the heart of an agency and the power of technology and data, we work with some of the world's most admired brands to help them on their transformation journey to becoming truly customer-centric. Together, we are 1000 + technology specialists, data scientists, strategic thinkers, consultants, operations experts, and creative minds from 55+ nationalities.

## A global network

We are part of the global VML network that encompasses more than 30,000 employees across 150+ offices in 60+ markets, each contributing to a culture that values connection, belonging, and the power of differences.

**Location:** London

• Ensure subcontractor documentation remains compliant and up to date.  
• Deliver against agreed KPIs and SLAs.

• Attend strategic and operational meetings with clients.

• Liaise with suppliers and subcontractors regarding pricing and service delivery.

• Develop and deliver training plans across engineering and management teams.

• Present contractual and financial performance to clients and BGIS leadership.  
• Manage staffing levels across contracts to support operational delivery.

• Promote learning, development and employee growth.

• Strong written and spoken English.
